title: Merchandise exports by continent of destination
subtitle: Figures correspond to the value of [merchandise exports](#dod:merchandise-exports) by continental destination as a share of [gross domestic product](#dod:gdp) (GDP). All partner countries are classified into continent groupings according to OWID's classification.
caption:
Merchandise exports by continent of destination

Figures correspond to the value of merchandise exports by continental destination as a share of gross domestic product (GDP). All partner countries are classified into continent groupings according to OWID's classification.

The stacked area chart displays India's merchandise exports as a share of GDP from 1949 to 2014, categorized by the continent of destination. The total height of the stacked areas represents the aggregate export share, which begins near 8% in 1949, declines to a low point below 4% around 1970, and subsequently rises to a peak exceeding 16% in the mid-2000s before settling between 16% and 17% in 2014. Exports to Asia, represented by the bottom blue band, constitute the largest component for most of the timeline, starting below 2%, dipping in the 1960s, and expanding significantly after 1990 to reach approximately 8% by the end of the period. The maroon band, indicating exports to Western Europe, remains a substantial layer throughout, generally fluctuating between 1% and 4%. Exports to North America, shown in green, form a consistent middle layer that widens notably after 1990, contributing roughly 2% to 4% to the total. The upper bands, representing exports to South America, Africa, Eastern Europe, and Oceania, appear as thinner layers. The series for Eastern Europe and Oceania show distinct, sharp spikes around 2008, where the total export share briefly surges above 18%. These estimates are derived from dyadic transactions data, with continent groupings following OWID's classification system.
